generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helper variables in AGREE expressions.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) match the expected expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` helpers to define reusable expressions within AGREE contracts.
Range checks typically use a boolean helper: `eq range_check : bool = x >= lower and x <= upper;`
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing unqualified identifiers or dependencies not present in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to add architecture to resolve AGREE type or reference errors; focus on fixing the expression.
Do not place guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
